heading: Program for pediatric studies of drugs
label: 284m Program for pediatric studies of drugs

children.heading: List of priority issues in pediatric therapeutics
children.label: a List of priority issues in pediatric therapeutics

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than one year after , the Secretary, acting through the Director of the National Institutes of Health and in consultation with the Commissioner of Food and Drugs and experts in pediatric research, shall develop and publish a priority list of needs in pediatric therapeutics, including drugs, biological products, or indications that require study. The list shall be revised every three years.September 27, 2007

children.label: i
children.text: therapeutic gaps in pediatrics that may include developmental pharmacology, pharmacogenetic determinants of drug response, metabolism of drugs and biologics in children, and pediatric clinical trials;

children.label: ii
children.text: particular pediatric diseases, disorders or conditions where more complete knowledge and testing of therapeutics, including drugs and biologics, and identification of biomarkers for such diseases, disorders, or conditions, may be beneficial in pediatric populations; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the adequacy of necessary infrastructure to conduct pediatric pharmacological research, including research networks and trained pediatric investigators; and

children.label: B
children.text: may consider the availability of qualified countermeasures (as defined in ), security countermeasures (as defined in ), and qualified pandemic or epidemic products (as defined in ) to address the needs of pediatric populations, in consultation with the Assistant Secretary for Preparedness and Response, consistent with the purposes of this section.section 247d–6a of this titlesection 247d–6b of this titlesection 247d–6d of this title

children.heading: Pediatric studies and research
children.label: b Pediatric studies and research
children.text: The Secretary, acting through the National Institutes of Health, shall award funds to entities that have the expertise to conduct pediatric clinical trials or other research (including qualified universities, hospitals, laboratories, contract research organizations, practice groups, federally funded programs such as pediatric pharmacology research units, other public or private institutions, or individuals) to enable the entities to conduct the drug studies or other research on the issues described in paragraphs (1) and (2)(A) of subsection (a). The Secretary may use contracts, grants, or other appropriate funding mechanisms to award funds under this subsection.

children.heading: Process for proposed pediatric study requests and labeling changes
children.label: c Process for proposed pediatric study requests and labeling changes

children.heading: Submission of proposed pediatric study request
children.label: 1 Submission of proposed pediatric study request
i
children.text: there is an approved application under section 505(j) of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act [] or ; or21 U.S.C. 355(j)section 262(k) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: there is a submitted application that could be approved under the criteria of such section; and

children.label: B
children.text: there remains no patent listed pursuant to section 505(b)(1) of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act [], and every three-year and five-year period referred to in subsection (c)(3)(E)(ii), (c)(3)(E)(iii), (c)(3)(E)(iv), (j)(5)(F)(ii), (j)(5)(F)(iii), or (j)(5)(F)(iv) of section 505 of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act [], or applicable twelve-year period referred to in , and any seven-year period referred to in section 527 of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act [] has ended for at least one form of the drug; and21 U.S.C. 355(b)(1)21 U.S.C. 355section 262(k)(7) of this title21 U.S.C. 360cc

children.label: C
children.text: additional studies are needed to assess the safety and effectiveness of the use of the drug in the pediatric population.

children.heading: Written request to holders of approved applications
children.label: 2 Written request to holders of approved applications
children.text: The Commissioner of Food and Drugs, in consultation with the Director of the National Institutes of Health, may issue a written request based on the proposed pediatric study request for the indication or indications submitted pursuant to paragraph (1) (which shall include a timeframe for negotiations for an agreement) for pediatric studies concerning a drug identified under subsection (a) to all holders of an approved application for the drug. Such a written request shall be made in a manner equivalent to the manner in which a written request is made under subsection (b) or (c) of section 505A of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act [] or , including with respect to information provided on the pediatric studies to be conducted pursuant to the request and using appropriate formulations for each age group for which the study is requested.21 U.S.C. 355asection 262(m) of this title

children.heading: Requests for proposals
children.label: 3 Requests for proposals
children.text: If the Commissioner of Food and Drugs does not receive a response to a written request issued under paragraph (2) not later than 30 days after the date on which a request was issued, the Secretary, acting through the Director of the National Institutes of Health and in consultation with the Commissioner of Food and Drugs, shall publish a request for proposals to conduct the pediatric studies described in the written request in accordance with subsection (b).

children.heading: Disqualification
children.label: 4 Disqualification
children.text: A holder that receives a first right of refusal shall not be entitled to respond to a request for proposals under paragraph (3).

children.heading: Contracts, grants, or other funding mechanisms
children.label: 5 Contracts, grants, or other funding mechanisms
children.text: A contract, grant, or other funding may be awarded under this section only if a proposal is submitted to the Secretary in such form and manner, and containing such agreements, assurances, and information as the Secretary determines to be necessary to carry out this section.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: On completion of a pediatric study in accordance with an award under this section, a report concerning the study shall be submitted to the Director of the National Institutes of Health and the Commissioner of Food and Drugs. The report shall include all data generated in connection with the study, including a written request if issued.

children.label: aa
children.text: human research participants, including with respect to privacy, security, informed consent, and protected health information; and

children.label: bb
children.text: proprietary interests, confidential commercial information, and intellectual property rights; and

children.label: II
children.text: assigned a docket number by the Commissioner of Food and Drugs and made available for the submission of public comments.

children.heading: Submission of comments
children.label: ii Submission of comments
children.text: An interested person may submit written comments concerning such pediatric studies to the Commissioner of Food and Drugs, and the submitted comments shall become part of the docket file with respect to each of the drugs.

children.heading: Action by Commissioner
children.label: C Action by Commissioner
children.text: The Commissioner of Food and Drugs shall take action in a timely and appropriate manner in response to the reports submitted under subparagraph (A), and shall begin such action upon receipt of the report under subparagraph (A), in accordance with paragraph (7).

children.label: A
children.text: review the report and such other data as are available concerning the safe and effective use in the pediatric population of the drug studied;

children.label: B
children.text: negotiate with the holders of approved applications for the drug studied for any labeling changes that the Commissioner of Food and Drugs determines to be appropriate and requests the holders to make; and

children.label: i
children.text: include in the public docket file a reference to the location of the report on the internet website of the National Institutes of Health and a copy of any requested labeling changes; and

children.label: ii
children.text: publish through a posting on the Web site of the Food and Drug Administration a summary of the report and a copy of any requested labeling changes.

children.heading: Referral to Pediatric Advisory Committee
children.label: A Referral to Pediatric Advisory Committee
children.text: If, not later than the end of the 180-day period specified in paragraph (7), the holder of an approved application for the drug involved does not agree to any labeling change requested by the Commissioner of Food and Drugs under that paragraph, the Commissioner of Food and Drugs shall refer the request to the Pediatric Advisory Committee.

children.heading: Action by the Pediatric Advisory Committee
children.label: B Action by the Pediatric Advisory Committee
i
children.text: review the available information on the safe and effective use of the drug in the pediatric population, including study reports submitted under this section; and

children.label: ii
children.text: make a recommendation to the Commissioner of Food and Drugs as to appropriate labeling changes, if any.

children.heading: FDA determination
children.label: 9 FDA determination
children.text: Not later than 30 days after receiving a recommendation from the Pediatric Advisory Committee under paragraph (8)(B)(ii) with respect to a drug, the Commissioner of Food and Drugs shall consider the recommendation and, if appropriate, make a request to the holders of approved applications for the drug to make any labeling change that the Commissioner of Food and Drugs determines to be appropriate.

children.heading: Failure to agree
children.label: 10 Failure to agree
children.text: If a holder of an approved application for a drug, within 30 days after receiving a request to make a labeling change under paragraph (9), does not agree to make a requested labeling change, the Commissioner of Food and Drugs may deem the drug to be misbranded under the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act [ et seq.].21 U.S.C. 301

children.heading: No effect on authority
children.label: 11 No effect on authority
children.text: Nothing in this subsection limits the authority of the United States to bring an enforcement action under the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act [ et seq.] when a drug lacks appropriate pediatric labeling. Neither course of action (the Pediatric Advisory Committee process or an enforcement action referred to in the preceding sentence) shall preclude, delay, or serve as the basis to stay the other course of action.21 U.S.C. 301

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section, $25,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2026 through 2028.

children.heading: Availability
children.label: 2 Availability
children.text: Any amount appropriated under paragraph (1) shall remain available to carry out this section until expended.
